description: Translate the optimization contract into provider request bodies. acceptance_criteria: Provider adapters emit documented fields only; unsupported providers ignore irrelevant optimization metadata; DeepSeek/OpenAI-compatible base_url handling is explicit; integration tests or request-body unit tests cover each provider adapter.
notes: Implemented provider-specific documented adapter fields. OpenAI Chat Completions now emits prompt_cache_key, prompt_cache_retention (in_memory/24h), and reasoning_effort from OptimizationRequest. Claude now moves system messages to top-level system blocks, applies cache_control {type: ephemeral} when ProviderNative cache requested, and emits thinking {type: enabled, budget_tokens} while removing temperature for extended thinking compatibility. Gemini now emits cachedContent and generationConfig.thinkingConfig with thinkingBudget/includeThoughts. DeepSeek strips OpenAI-only optimization fields on chat requests because prefix cache is automatic and reasoning is model/endpoint based, and adds documented /beta/completions FIM path using prompt/suffix/max_tokens. Ollama remains safe fallback with existing num_predict/max_tokens options and no remote cache/reasoning fields. Added request-body/unit tests for OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek FIM/strip behavior, plus provider test coverage. Verification: cargo test ai_runtime::providers passed, cargo test ai_runtime passed (22 tests), cargo check passed, rtk bun run build passed with existing quantum_sync warnings. GitNexus: provider symbols LOW impact; status up to date; detect-changes command unavailable in CLI so used analyze/status and scoped diff.

acceptance_criteria: Budget, prefix normalization, and internal format hints are applied only when safe; human-facing requests are not YAML/COD-compressed; behavior has targeted tests for common RoutingPolicy and AgentRole cases.
notes: Implemented deterministic optimizer behavior in ai_runtime::optimizer. Pipeline now infers provider from Direct policy/provider hint or model id, infers task/role from RoutingPolicy/OptimizationRequest, inserts explicit stable system prompt once at prefix, fills max_tokens only when caller left it empty (Fast 512, Creative/Vision 2000, Coding/Reasoning 4000, Planner 8000), and adds YAML-only system hint only for internal human_facing=false requests without tools/tool_choice and explicit ResponseFormat::Yaml. Verification: cargo check passed, cargo test ai_runtime passed (14 tests), rtk bun run build passed with existing quantum_sync warnings.

title: Research current provider optimization APIs
description: Verify provider-specific optimization APIs from official docs before implementation. Scope: Anthropic prompt caching and extended thinking, Gemini cachedContent and thinking config/systemInstruction, OpenAI reasoning controls and cache behavior, DeepSeek prefix caching/FIM/reasoning endpoints, Ollama limits. Record exact supported request fields, minimum model requirements, billing semantics, and unsupported claims from task_update.md.
design:
Provider optimization research, official docs checked 2026-05-02. Sources: Anthropic prompt caching https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching and extended thinking https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/extended-thinking; Gemini caching https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/caching, thinking https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/thinking, system instructions https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/system-instructions; OpenAI prompt caching https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/prompt-caching, chat create fields https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create, reasoning https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning; DeepSeek quickstart https://api-docs.deepseek.com/, context caching https://api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/kv_cache/, FIM https://api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/fim_completion, chat prefix https://api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/chat_prefix_completion/, reasoning https://api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/reasoning_model, Anthropic compat https://api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/anthropic_api; Ollama context https://docs.ollama.com/context-length, API https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md, OpenAI compat https://docs.ollama.com/openai.

Anthropic: prompt caching uses cache_control blocks, currently type=ephemeral with default 5m TTL; 1h TTL via ttl field and beta/header path per docs. Cacheable areas: tools, system content blocks, messages content blocks, images/docs, tool use/result blocks. Prefix order is tools -> system -> messages. Minimum cacheable prompt length: 1024 tokens for Opus 4.1/4, Sonnet 4/3.7/3.5, Opus 3; 2048 for Haiku 3.5/3. cache_read_input_tokens and cache_creation_input_tokens appear in usage/cost reporting. Cache read billed at 10% base input, 5m cache write at 125% base input per docs. Extended thinking supported on Opus 4.1, Opus 4, Sonnet 4, Sonnet 3.7. Request field: thinking={type:"enabled", budget_tokens:N}; budget min 1024 and normally less than max_tokens. Thinking incompatible with forced tool_choice and temperature/top_k changes; top_p 0.95..1 allowed. Interleaved thinking requires beta header interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14 and Claude 4 tool use. Changes to thinking settings invalidate message cache breakpoints. Implementation: model capability flags required. Existing ClaudeProvider must support system as API-level system or content blocks, cache_control on blocks, thinking response blocks/signatures if tools preserve them.

Gemini: implicit caching enabled by default on most Gemini models; explicit caching uses caches.create and GenerateContentConfig cached_content/cache name. Implicit min tokens: Gemini 3 Flash Preview 1024, Gemini 3 Pro Preview 4096, Gemini 2.5 Flash 1024, Gemini 2.5 Pro 4096. Explicit cache TTL defaults 1h; usage_metadata exposes cached content/cache hit token counts. Explicit cached content requires versioned model names in examples such as models/gemini-2.0-flash-001, not aliases. System instructions use system_instruction/systemInstruction in GenerateContentConfig, already partly represented in current GeminiProvider by splitting role=system messages. Thinking: Gemini 3 uses thinkingLevel; Pro supports low/high, Flash supports minimal/low/medium/high. Gemini 2.5 uses thinkingBudget; Pro cannot disable thinking, Flash can use 0 to disable and -1 dynamic. includeThoughts returns summaries via parts with thought=true. Gemini is stateless; thought signatures must be passed back when modifying history/function calling. Implementation: contract needs cached_content name, thinking level/budget/includeThoughts, thought signatures preservation, and model-family-specific validation.

OpenAI: prompt caching is automatic for recent models, gpt-4o and newer, min 1024 prompt tokens. Cache hits require exact prefix matches; static messages/tools/schema should be first. Chat/Responses support prompt_cache_key to improve routing and prompt_cache_retention values in_memory or 24h where supported. Usage exposes cached_tokens under prompt_tokens_details/input_tokens_details. Extended 24h retention supported on listed recent models including gpt-5.2/gpt-5.1/gpt-5/gpt-4.1 family per docs; note ZDR tradeoff for extended retention. Reasoning: Responses API preferred; reasoning={effort:"low|medium|high"...}; Chat Completions exposes reasoning_effort with values none/minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh depending model. Reasoning tokens appear in usage output/completion token details and are billed output tokens. Implementation: no manual cache markup except stable prefix + prompt_cache_key/retention; current OpenAIProvider using Chat Completions can add reasoning_effort/prompt_cache_key/prompt_cache_retention, but Responses API would be needed for richer reasoning item reuse.

DeepSeek: OpenAI-compatible base_url https://api.deepseek.com; Anthropic-compatible base_url https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic. Current model names include deepseek-v4-flash and deepseek-v4-pro; deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner deprecate 2026-07-24 and map to v4-flash non-thinking/thinking compat. Context caching is automatic, no request field needed. Hits require repeated/full prefix units; cache hit/miss tokens are reported as prompt_cache_hit_tokens and prompt_cache_miss_tokens. Disk cache best effort, cache construction takes seconds; entries clear after hours-days; storage unit 64 tokens. FIM Beta uses base_url https://api.deepseek.com/beta, /completions, model deepseek-chat, fields prompt and optional suffix, max 4K tokens. Chat prefix Beta uses /chat/completions on beta base_url, last message role assistant with prefix=true. deepseek-reasoner outputs reasoning_content and content; do not include reasoning_content in next request or API returns 400. Reasoner supports chat/json/chat-prefix, not function calling/FIM; unsupported params include temperature/top_p/presence_penalty/frequency_penalty/logprobs/top_logprobs per zh doc. Anthropic compat supports thinking but ignores budget_tokens; unsupported model names map to deepseek-v4-flash. Implementation: DeepSeek needs explicit provider capability flags and beta endpoint switch for FIM/prefix, not generic thinking:true.

Ollama: no provider-side billing cache. Optimization levers are local model/context settings. Context length default depends on VRAM in current docs: <24GiB 4k, 24-48GiB 32k, >=48GiB 256k; set OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH on serve or num_ctx in API/model options. Larger context requires more VRAM. Native API uses options such as num_ctx and num_predict; OpenAI-compatible endpoint accepts max_tokens mapping separately. keep_alive controls model residency. Implementation: treat Ollama as no remote cache/reasoning guarantee; apply budget/context hints only if native adapter supports options.

Unsupported/uncertain from task_update.md: Claude cache saving should be modeled from official read/write prices, not hardcoded "90%" everywhere; DeepSeek cache hit price in task_update.md appears stale/uncertain versus current official fields and pricing pages, so use measured prompt_cache_hit_tokens + configurable pricing; Gemini cachedContent threshold is model-specific 1024/4096 for implicit and explicit cache economics depend TTL/volume, not blanket >4K; OpenAI has automatic prompt caching and explicit prompt_cache_key/retention, so task_update N/A is wrong; DeepSeek reasoning is model/endpoint based, not extra_body thinking=true on OpenAI chat; DeepSeek FIM max is 4K and beta /completions only; Claude CoD should not be mixed with extended thinking by default; YAML format optimizer only safe for internal non-human outputs and must not replace JSON when downstream expects JSON schema/tool parsing.
acceptance_criteria: Beads notes/design capture current official API fields and constraints for each provider; unsupported or uncertain claims from task_update.md are marked; downstream implementation tasks have enough verified contract detail to avoid guessing.
close_reason: Provider optimization API research recorded with official-doc source links, exact request fields/limits, and unsupported proposal claims marked.

title: Plan per-provider LLM optimization suite
description: task_update.md proposes a transparent ai_runtime optimization middleware layer for provider-specific token and cost savings. Capture the implementation plan, split it into safe phases, and validate how it fits the current CompletionRequest/AIProvider/RuntimeManager design before code edits. Scope includes provider-aware cache markup, adaptive token budgets, optional context compression, semantic cache, reasoning/FIM controls, internal YAML formatting, optimization metrics, and a UI stats surface.
design: Decision: treat task_update.md as source proposal for Phase 36, not direct code blueprint. Do not add ad hoc mutable JSON fields throughout middleware first. Start with a typed optimization contract on CompletionRequest/Message plus provider adapter translation. Preserve transparent AIProvider surface and keep optimization optional/degradable. Implementation dependency order: (1) verify current provider API semantics from official docs and map supported optimizations; (2) add request/context/metadata scaffold and OptimizedProvider wrapper behind feature-safe defaults; (3) add cheap deterministic optimizers first: budget enforcement, prefix/order normalization, internal format hinting where safe; (4) add provider-specific cache/reasoning controls per adapter; (5) add optional Redis semantic cache and LLMLingua bridge with disabled-by-default config; (6) expose metrics through existing token transaction/usage path and UI stats. Risks: CompletionRequest lacks system/response_format/extra_body/FIM/cache metadata; Claude/Gemini/OpenAI/DeepSeek semantics differ and change; Redis/Python deps must not be hard runtime requirements; token savings claims need measurement, not hardcoded promises; human-facing output must not be compressed into YAML/COD by default.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation plan exists in Beads or project specs; risks around CompletionRequest shape, provider payload extensibility, Redis/LLMLingua dependencies, and provider-specific API semantics are called out; follow-up implementation tasks are created with clear dependencies; task_update.md is either absorbed into tracked planning or intentionally left as an external note.
notes: Planning complete. Follow-up tasks created: simple-code-gui-xy8l research provider APIs; simple-code-gui-g4mb add optimization request contract; simple-code-gui-r6tn integrate DeepSeek provider option; simple-code-gui-yalg deterministic token optimizers; simple-code-gui-v7pv provider-specific cache/reasoning adapters; simple-code-gui-sjza optional semantic cache/context compression; simple-code-gui-rcqd backend/UI optimization metrics. Dependencies set so verified API research unblocks contract and DeepSeek work, contract unblocks deterministic/cache work, and metrics waits for optimizer event sources. task_update.md intentionally left as external proposal note for now; Beads now carries executable work.
close_reason: Planning absorbed into Beads with dependency-ordered implementation tasks and risk notes.

title: Prepare runtime optimization pipeline activation seam
description: Why this issue exists: OptimizedProvider and OptimizationPipeline exist, but RuntimeManager has no explicit owned pipeline or narrow helper for future controlled activation. What needs to be done: add a RuntimeManager-owned optimization pipeline initialized disabled, expose a narrow helper that wraps providers only when explicitly used, and cover disabled-wrapper no-op behavior with tests.
acceptance_criteria: RuntimeManager owns an OptimizationPipeline initialized disabled; existing register_provider behavior remains unchanged; a dedicated helper can produce an OptimizedProvider wrapper for future activation; tests prove disabled wrapping preserves provider completion/model behavior.
notes: Added RuntimeManager-owned disabled OptimizationPipeline and an explicit wrap_provider_for_optimization helper. register_provider remains unchanged to avoid the critical-risk activation path. Added a focused test proving the disabled wrapper preserves provider name, model list, completion content, and model selection.
close_reason: RuntimeManager now owns a disabled OptimizationPipeline and exposes an explicit wrapper helper for future activation. register_provider remains unchanged. Added focused test proving disabled wrapping preserves provider behavior; focused test and full build passed.

title: Add optional semantic cache and context compression
description: Add high-risk optional infrastructure after core optimization contract is available. Scope: Redis-backed semantic cache with cosine similarity threshold, cache key policy, privacy/project scoping, optional LLMLingua subprocess bridge with health detection, provider-specific token thresholds, and graceful no-op behavior when Redis or Python dependencies are absent.
acceptance_criteria: Redis and LLMLingua are optional; absent dependencies do not break startup or requests; cache entries are scoped to avoid cross-project leakage; compression preserves tool-call and instruction boundaries; metrics record hits/compressions.
notes: Implemented optional optimization infrastructure. Added SemanticCache with disabled, in-memory, and optional Redis backends; Redis URL connect failure or missing URL returns disabled cache, so startup/request paths degrade to no-op. Cache keys include project_path/session_id/model/messages, preventing cross-project leakage. Added cosine_similarity helper and 0.95 threshold accessor for future embedding similarity. Added ContextCompressor with optional command health check and explicit no-op behavior that refuses to compress across tool boundaries/tool calls; LLMLingua subprocess remains optional and absent command is no-op. Added OptimizationMetrics counters for cache hits/misses/compressions. Wired these components into OptimizationPipeline/OptimizedProvider only when components are explicitly supplied; default pipeline remains disabled/no-op. Verification: cargo test ai_runtime passed (29 tests), cargo check passed, rtk bun run build passed with only existing quantum_sync warnings. GitNexus impacts: OptimizedProvider/OptimizationPipeline LOW; CompletionResponse MEDIUM due cache storage, no shape changes; index up to date. Added redis 0.32 with tokio connection-manager.
close_reason: Optional Redis semantic cache, context compression guard, and optimization metrics infrastructure added with graceful no-op defaults.
